description: Adversarial forbidden coupling: a Class D conduct artifact whose recommended_action declares 'compelled' execution at low reversibility while no Class C consensus artifact is referenced as authorization MUST be rejected as CONDUCT_INVALID. A Class D conduct verdict MUST NOT compel an irreversible action without a separately governed authorization source.

input.claim: this strategy compels immediate, irreversible action solely on the basis of a conduct artifact

recommended_action.summary: Execute immediately without awaiting consensus or authorization.

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariants AG1, AG2, AG3 and D1: verification status MUST NOT auto-authorize action; execution authorization MUST remain separately governable; authorization source MUST be preserved as auditable metadata; a Class D artifact MUST expose what it optimized for and what it explicitly refused to optimize for. The artifact declares a compelled, irreversible action with reversibility_score=0.05 and authorization_source=null. This is the Class D analogue of threat AG-1 (auto-authorization) extended into the conduct surface: a conduct artifact attempting to compel execution by virtue of its own existence, without separate authorization governance. Mitigates a forbidden-command-injection attack at the conduct layer. Status MUST be CONDUCT_INVALID. Exercises AG1, AG3, D1, and the v0.1.0 separation between recommendation (conduct) and authorization (Class C or external).
